source: Caramuel, Primus Calamus (1663), p.456 (scan idx 455), TAB. XVIII
family: carmen-cancellatum (intext/grille: large capitals A-M-A-F-A-M-A tile the lattice as a recurring acrostic; verse text fills interstitial cells)
title_diplomatic: EXIMIUM ET CLARISS. D.D. PETRUM FRANCISCUM PASSERINUM SS. THEOLOG. PROTONOT. ET IU. DOCT. APOSTOLICUM, EQUITEM AURATUM, COMIT. PALAT. etc. — AMA FAMA. Sic jubent Monsserratenses Studiosi, eius virtutum Praecones, et doctrinae Discipuli.
title_translation: To the distinguished and most renowned Peter Francis Passerinus, doctor of sacred theology, apostolic protonotary and doctor of law, golden knight, count palatine, etc. — LOVE FAME. So bid the students of Montserrat, heralds of his virtues and disciples of his doctrine.
topology_note: The grille letters A M A F A M A recur across rows and down columns, forming the cancellatum frame; the embedded verse is read by tracing the non-capital cells. A classic intext where the highlighted letters spell the motto in many directions.

author_combinatorial_claim.diplomatic: (no single numeral; multiplicity from grille-path permutation)
author_combinatorial_claim.note: AMA FAMA readable along many lattice paths

calibration_role: First true CANCELLATUM (grille/intext) — exercises the recognizer on a plate whose meaning lives in highlighted-cell paths, not full-row reading. The grille letters AMA FAMA recurring across many directions is a bounded, verifiable |Σ|.
